items.title: Defamation case involving former Carlton president Luke Sayers to be heard publicly after failed court bid

items.title: New political candidates to benefit under changes to Victoria's electoral act
items.summary: Six months out from the state election, Victoria had no rules governing donations due to the High Court finding in April that the laws were unconstitutional.

items.title: Sheepskin market dramatically rebounds amid tighter supply and Chinese demand
items.summary: Just 12 months ago, Australian sheepskins were worthless and being dumped in landfill. They are now in hot demand amid a surging merino wool industry.

items.title: Barnaby Joyce speaks at Sydney rally ahead of bill to outlaw sex-selective abortions
items.summary: Nine months out from the NSW election campaign, One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce has appeared in Sydney, declaring politicians are fearful of losing their jobs.

items.title: Medicare bulk-billing model forces Kalbarri Doctors Surgery closure, director says
items.summary: Kalbarri residents will be forced to travel more than 100 kilometres to visit a doctor when the regional WA town's only GP clinic closes due to financial pressure.

items.title: Donald Trump appoints ally Bill Pulte as acting US intelligence director
items.summary: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

items.title: Young cancer patient's stem cell transplant struggle highlights shortage of local donors
items.summary: Sarah Taylor is one of approximately 1,200 Australians urgently waiting for a life-saving stem cell transplant. Advocates say Australia's registry is "ridiculously small" compared to other nations, leading to potentially fatal consequences.

items.title: Australia levels ODI series in Pakistan as Nathan Ellis stars with the ball

items.title: Red skies, rainbows and a ravaging storm — see WA's best weather photos for May
items.summary: May delivered Western Australia bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies that left many wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang.

items.title: Starmer condemns Henry Nowak's death as police response sparks Southampton race protests
items.summary: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

items.title: PM labels Taylor 'Temu Abbott' as Coalition turns up heat on economy — as it happened
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faces a grilling from the opposition over Australia's economy after new figures show a slowdown in growth.

items.title: Marta Kostyuk praises Daria Kasatkina ahead of French Open semifinal against Russia's Mirra Andreeva

items.title: Reports of wildlife displaced at Fred Caterson Reserve as controversial sports project begins

items.title: Mango twig tip dieback spreading in the NT as more trees get bulldozed

items.title: SA Voice damaged by advertising failures and voter confusion, FOI documents show

items.title: Quilpie builders experiment with 'flat-pack' housing to improve supply in the outback

items.title: Tasmanians letting fitness regimes slip as cost-of-living pressures rise

items.title: Billions over budget and years late: How Snowy 2.0 got here

items.title: We asked Google AI about big tobacco. Its answer was missing some controversial history

items.title: Australians have become housing hostages. Will the budget set them free?
items.summary: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

items.title: Trump withdraws $2.5b 'weaponization' fund following 'jaw-dropping' corruption allegations
items.summary: The US president has abandoned plans for a $2.51 billion "anti-weaponization" fund for ‌victims of alleged government "weaponization", according to the acting attorney-general.

items.title: Three Australian men found guilty after taking 'black flight' into Indonesia's South Papua province

items.title: Australian-made rocket motor designed for missiles test-fired for first time

items.title: Almost eight months on from the ceasefire, Gaza's residents are still struggling to rebuild a city in ruins
items.summary: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.

items.summary: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

items.title: Steady is a joy, but raising a dog wasn't what I'd imagined
items.summary: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.
